heroHeadline: Homalin River Port Fire Kills 2 in Sagaing — Second Port Disaster in 2026; April 20 SAC Hospital Bombing Crosses Into Thailand; KIA Kills ~40 Militia in Hpakant
heroSubtitle: On April 21, a major fire and explosion at the Homalin river port (Sagaing region) killed 2 and injured 11, destroying 22 boats — the second deadly port fire in Homalin in 2026, underscoring the cumulative infrastructure collapse in conflict-ravaged Sagaing. The previous day, on April 20, Myanmar Air Force Yak-130 and MiG-29 jets dropped nine bombs on a KNU hospital in Bue So (Karen State) — one bomb crossed into Thailand's Mae Hong Son; 6 Arakan Army members were killed in the internationally condemned strike. On April 19, KIA forces killed approximately 40 pro-junta militia in a landmine ambush in Hpakant; the SAC bombed Mrauk-U and Ponnagyun in Rakhine State injuring 13 civilians. The Inter-Parliamentary Union on April 19 reaffirmed CRPH recognition as Myanmar's principal parliamentary body, rejecting the junta's December 2025 elections. On April 18, KIA captured two militia bases in Tanai Township. The Thingyan festival saw 110+ aerial attacks on Ayadaw Township; TNLA's April 15 statement fracturing the Three Brotherhood Alliance remains the key political fault line. Day 1,905 of the conflict.
footerNote: Data sourced from AAPP, ACLED, UN OCHA, Irrawaddy, RFA, Myanmar Now, DVB, Al Jazeera, Mizzima, Human Rights Watch, Crisis Group, Myanmar Peace Monitor, MoeMaKa, Khaosod English, The Nation Thailand, Xinhua, BERNAMA, Anadolu Agency, IMNA/Mon News, FDD, and official NUG, KNU, KIA, and AA statements.
